english_summary: Mark Zuckerberg is reportedly working on an AI-powered clone of himself, which would appear in an advanced 3D format and hold meetings with Meta employees on his behalf. The article also notes that US tech giants are pouring billions into AI ventures whose full scope and consequences remain unclear. The piece is a commentary by journalist Michael Jarlner published in the Danish newspaper Politiken.
danish_text_clean:
Facebook-stifteren Mark Zuckerberg er i gang med at finde en afløser: En kunstigt intelligent klon af ham selv.

Klonen vil efter planen optræde i et avanceret 3D-format og kan derefter holde møder med medarbejderne i Facebook-moderselskabet, Meta.

USA's teknologigiganter sprøjter milliarder ind i et ai-eventyr, som ingen kan overskue hverken omfanget eller konsekvensen af. Natten til torsdag kom der nye vilde tal, fortæller Michael Jarlner om i denne kommentar.

vocabulary.example_from_article: Facebook-stifteren Mark Zuckerberg er i gang med at finde en afløser.
vocabulary.note: en-word (en stifter, stifteren). Common suffix: -er = person who does something, like 'lærer' (teacher).

vocabulary.example_from_article: Mark Zuckerberg er i gang med at finde en afløser.
vocabulary.note: en-word. From the verb 'afløse' (to relieve, replace). Also used for a substitute worker or successor.

vocabulary.example_from_article: En kunstigt intelligent klon af ham selv.
vocabulary.note: Adverbial form of 'kunstig'. 'Kunstig intelligens' = artificial intelligence (AI). Note: 'kunst' alone means 'art'.

vocabulary.english: to appear, to perform, to act
vocabulary.example_from_article: Klonen vil efter planen optræde i et avanceret 3D-format.
vocabulary.note: Separable verb: op + træde. In formal or media contexts means 'to appear/feature'. Also used for stage performances.

vocabulary.example_from_article: Klonen vil efter planen optræde i et avanceret 3D-format.
vocabulary.note: Past participle of 'avancere' used as adjective. Borrowed from French. Common in tech contexts.

vocabulary.example_from_article: holde møder med medarbejderne i Facebook-moderselskabet, Meta.
vocabulary.note: et-word, definite: moderselskabet. Compound: 'moder' (mother) + 'selskab' (company). Danish frequently builds long compound nouns this way.

vocabulary.example_from_article: USA's teknologigiganter sprøjter milliarder ind i et ai-eventyr.
vocabulary.note: en-word, plural: teknologigiganter. Triple compound: teknologi + gi + gant. The plural of en-words typically adds -er or -e.

vocabulary.english: to inject, to pump, to spray
vocabulary.example_from_article: USA's teknologigiganter sprøjter milliarder ind i et ai-eventyr.
vocabulary.note: Here used figuratively: 'sprøjte penge ind i noget' = to pump money into something. Literally means to spray or inject a liquid.

vocabulary.english: to comprehend, to have a full overview of, to manage
vocabulary.example_from_article: som ingen kan overskue hverken omfanget eller konsekvensen af.
vocabulary.note: Tricky word: 'overskue' does NOT mean 'overlook' (ignore) as in English. It means to be able to survey or grasp something fully. A partial false friend.

vocabulary.example_from_article: som ingen kan overskue hverken omfanget eller konsekvensen af.
vocabulary.note: et-word, definite: omfanget. From 'om' + 'fang' (grasp). Very common in formal and journalistic Danish.
In everyday and journalistic Danish, 'vild' is commonly used as slang for 'amazing' or 'shocking'. Here: 'vilde tal' = staggering/mind-blowing numbers.

vocabulary.example_from_article: kan derefter holde møder med medarbejderne i Facebook-moderselskabet.
vocabulary.note: en-word. Definite plural: medarbejderne. Compound: 'med' (with) + 'arbejder' (worker). More neutral/respectful than 'ansatte' (the employed).

grammar_notes.explanation: Danish strings nouns together into single words without spaces, often creating very long compounds. This article has several examples: 'Facebook-stifteren' (Facebook founder), 'moderselskabet' (parent company), 'teknologigiganter' (tech giants), and 'ai-eventyr' (AI adventure). The last element determines the gender and plural form of the whole compound.

'Vil' is used here in 'Klonen vil efter planen optræde i et avanceret 3D-format.' Danish uses 'vil' both as a future marker (will) and to express wanting/intending. In this sentence it signals a planned future event. Unlike English, Danish has no separate '-ing' form, so 'er i gang med at finde' (is in the process of finding) uses the infinitive with 'at'.

Danish marks definiteness by adding a suffix to the noun rather than a separate word: 'en stifter' (a founder) → 'stifteren' (the founder); 'et selskab' (a company) → 'selskabet' (the company). When an adjective precedes the noun, a separate 'den/det/de' is added as well (double definiteness), but in this article most definite nouns appear without a preceding adjective.

discussion_questions: Hvad synes du om idéen med en kunstig klon af Zuckerberg? Er det en god idé for virksomheder?
Hvad betyder det, at 'ingen kan overskue konsekvensen' af ai? Kan du give et eksempel?
Kender du andre teknologigiganter end Meta? Hvad laver de?
title: Mark Zuckerberg har store planer, men han har lige fået en lussing
